If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). 2026-03-25T07:30:41.2926112Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-25T07:30:26.658232331Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57449,\"title\":\"Generator: internal deserialization constructor should initialize collection properties\",\"body\":\"## Description\\n\\nThe mgmt generator emits an internal parameterless constructor for deserialization that does **not** initialize collection properties (e.g., `IList`), leaving them as `null`. When a parent model lazily creates an instance via this constructor and then accesses the collection property, it results in a `NullReferenceException`.\\n\\n## Repro\\n\\nGiven a TypeSpec model like:\\n\\n```typespec\\nmodel ManagementPolicySchema {\\n  rules: ManagementPolicyRule[];\\n}\\n```\\n\\nThe generator produces:\\n\\n```csharp\\ninternal partial class ManagementPolicySchema\\n{\\n    // Public constructor - correctly initializes Rules\\n    public ManagementPolicySchema(IEnumerable rules)\\n    {\\n        Rules = rules.ToList();\\n    }\\n\\n    // Internal deserialization constructor - does NOT initialize Rules\\n    internal ManagementPolicySchema()\\n    {\\n        // Rules remains null\\n    }\\n\\n    public IList Rules { get; }\\n}\\n```\\n\\nWhen a parent model lazily creates `ManagementPolicySchema` via the parameterless constructor:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// In ManagementPolicyProperties (generated code)\\npublic IList Rules\\n{\\n    get\\n    {\\n        if (Policy is null)\\n        {\\n            Policy = new ManagementPolicySchema(); // uses parameterless ctor\\n        }\\n        return Policy.Rules; // NullReferenceException!\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n## Expected Behavior\\n\\nThe internal deserialization constructor should initialize collection properties to empty collections (e.g., `new ChangeTrackingList()`), consistent with how the public constructor initializes them.\\n\\n```csharp\\ninternal ManagementPolicySchema()\\n{\\n    Rules = new ChangeTrackingList();\\n}\\n```\\n\\n## Actual Behavior\\n\\nCollection properties remain `null`, causing `NullReferenceException` when accessed through lazy initialization patterns.\\n\\n## Workaround\\n\\nCurrently worked around in Azure.ResourceManager.Storage by using the public constructor with an empty list:\\n\\n```csharp\\nPolicy = new ManagementPolicySchema(new ChangeTrackingList());\\n```\\n\\n## Context\\n\\nFound during Azure.ResourceManager.Storage migration from AutoRest to TypeSpec.\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57449\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"live1206\",\"id\":5196139,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/live1206\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5196139?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"bug\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-03-25T07:28:19Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-25T07:28:19Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
2026-03-25T07:30:41.2936813Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-25T07:30:26.669367197Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"issue_number":57449,"method":"get","owner":"Azure","repo":"azure-sdk-for-net"},"name":"issue_read"}}}

When a parent model lazily creates an instance via this constructor and then accesses the collection property, it results in a `NullReferenceException`.\\n\\n## Repro\\n\\nGiven a TypeSpec model like:\\n\\n```typespec\\nmodel ManagementPolicySchema {\\n  rules: ManagementPolicyRule[];\\n}\\n```\\n\\nThe generator produces:\\n\\n```csharp\\ninternal partial class ManagementPolicySchema\\n{\\n    // Public constructor - correctly initializes Rules\\n    public ManagementPolicySchema(IEnumerable rules)\\n    {\\n        Rules = rules.ToList();\\n    }\\n\\n    // Internal deserialization constructor - does NOT initialize Rules\\n    internal ManagementPolicySchema()\\n    {\\n        // Rules remains null\\n    }\\n\\n    public IList Rules { get; // uses parameterless ctor\\n        }\\n        return Policy.Rules; // NullReferenceException!\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n## Expected Behavior\\n\\nThe internal deserialization constructor should initialize collection properties to empty collections (e.g., `new ChangeTrackingList()`), consistent with how the public constructor initializes them.\\n\\n```csharp\\ninternal ManagementPolicySchema()\\n{\\n    Rules = new ChangeTrackingList();\\n}\\n```\\n\\n## Actual Behavior\\n\\nCollection properties remain `null`, causing `NullReferenceException` when accessed through lazy initialization patterns.\\n\\n## Workaround\\n\\nCurrently worked around in Azure.ResourceManager.Storage by using the public constructor with an empty list:\\n\\n```csharp\\nPolicy = new ManagementPolicySchema(new ChangeTrackingList());\\n```\\n\\n## Context\\n\\nFound during Azure.ResourceManager.Storage migration from AutoRest to TypeSpec.\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57449\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"live1206\",\"id\":5196139,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/live1206\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5196139?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"bug\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-03-25T07:28:19Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-25T07:28:19Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
